---
title: "The use of AI-based tools in Bioinformatics"
subtitle: "Retreat de Bioinformáticos (CIBERONC)"
date: "April 10, 2026"
author: "Daniel González Camblor"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, eval = FALSE)
```

---

## Requirements

- **Google account**. You will need a Google account to access most of the tools used throughout the workshop. If you do not already have one, create one at [accounts.google.com](https://accounts.google.com). Make sure the account is fully verified (phone number confirmed).

- **Visual Studio Code**. One of the most popular code editors. Implements GitHub Copilot, an extension that provides basic AI coding assistance. You can download it from [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com), and then install the GitHub Copilot extension from the Extensions Marketplace. [Free for students](https://education.github.com/pack), limited use for non-students. GitHub Copilot is also integrated in RStudio.

- **Antigravity**. Antigravity is an agent-focused code editor, although it also offers in-line coding assistance like GitHub Copilot. It has a generous free tier, and its interface is similar to VS Code (actually a fork of it). You can get it from [here](https://antigravity.google/). An alternative is Cursor, but we won't be using it in the workshop.

Meeting these requirements is not strictly necessary to follow the workshop, but it will allow you to play along and get the most out of it.

---

## Outline

### 1. Introduction

#### 1.1 AI in Bioinformatics  
#### 1.2 General Tips  

### 2. Research Basics: From an Idea to an Actionable Plan

#### 2.1 Drafting Ideas  
**Tools:** Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT  

#### 2.2 Searching the Literature  
**Tool:** Perplexity  

#### 2.3 Summarizing the Literature  
**Tool:** NotebookLM  

### 3. Coding with AI Assistants (Basics)

#### 3.1 Chat-Based Tools vs. Coding Assistants  

#### 3.2 The Use of the Inline Copilot and the Chat-Based Copilot
**Tool:** Visual Studio Code, RStudio  

### 4. Coding with AI Assistants (Medium)

#### 4.1 Creating a Pipeline from Scratch: AI Agents 
**Tool:** Antigravity  

### 5. Coding with AI Assistants (Advanced)

#### 5.1 Personalizing Your Agent  
**Tool:** Antigravity  

### 6. Downstream Applications of Chat-Based AI  
**Tools:** Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT   

### 7. The Risks of AI

### 8. Next Steps and Conclusion
